organizations.org: Illinois assault weapons ban (Protect Illinois Communities Act) — legislative support
organizations.role: Supporting state legislator / public advocate for the ban
organizations.notes: Corroborated as Kambium Elijah 'Kam' Buckner, Democratic member of the Illinois House from the 26th District. Three independent points match the filing: the reported employer/title (fields swapped) matches his documented role as VP of governmental affairs at OUTFRONT Media since 2015, and zip 60653 is Bronzeville, a neighborhood explicitly within his 26th District. He is a public official, not a private individual; only his publicly documented roles are recorded here. No Texas lobbyist registration found (he is an Illinois official); no federal PAC contributions surfaced — his FEC-adjacent footprint is as a recipient of state campaign funds, not an ideological PAC donor.

organizations.org: Moms Demand Action for Gun Sense in America — Texas chapter
organizations.notes: Nadia Barbot is an Austin technology product manager (Group Product Manager at Sysco LABS; formerly Bazaarvoice) who serves as Secretary of the Pecan Springs-Springdale Hills Neighborhood Association — the association covering the 78723 Mueller-area zip on the filing, which corroborates the identity directly. Her heavy federal donation history (77 contributions, lean 1.0) is consistent with her documented gun-safety advocacy; no individual PAC-level records were retrievable through public search tools, and no TEC lobbyist registration was found.

Longtime Austin civic figure and philanthropist who runs MDW Interests, a private oil, gas and real estate investment firm registered in Austin. Co-founder and president of the Waller Creek Conservancy (now Waterloo Greenway), former facilities chair at St. Edward's University, and instrumental in the merger creating The Contemporary Austin. Despite the 'Unemployed / None' occupation on the filing, her business identity is documented in public press releases. Her 213 federal contributions with a near-uniform Democratic lean are consistent with the civic-donor profile; no ideological PAC tie (pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, gun, or defense) was found in public records.

organizations.org: Tabrizi v. City of Austin (land-use litigation)
organizations.notes: Dona Avery Tabrizi of 1102 Claire Ave, Austin TX 78703 (matches filing zip); named plaintiff with spouse Ali Tabrizi in Tabrizi v. City of Austin (Tex. App. 2018), a land-use suit over a building permit on a .56-acre remainder lot the couple bought in 2013, litigated against the City's Planning/Zoning, Development Services and Environmental officers. Public records list Tabrizi and Co Inc and Tabrizi Investments LLC (NAICS: lessors of residential buildings and dwellings) at the same address. Spouse Ali Tabrizi is an Austin angel/VC investor (CTAN, LiveOak, Capital Factory). She reports her own occupation as unemployed, so she is classified Not Employed; the real-estate and land-use exposure is household-level, not a personal employer.

organizations.role: Former board member and community relations / marketing executive (through the 2019 sale)
organizations.notes: Lynne Dobson is an Austin/West Lake Hills philanthropist and former photojournalist married to Greg Wooldridge; she is a member of the Dobson family that owned Whataburger and served on its board until the 2019 sale. Identity corroborated by a City of Austin board-nomination document listing her at a 78746 address and by FEC records showing a Lynne Dobson, self-employed photographer, Austin TX 78746.

Jonathan Friedmann is a licensed Austin real estate agent with Friedmann Parks Realty, whose business address (1310 Bentwood Rd, Austin, TX 78722) is an exact match to the donor's filing zip; 16 years in the business, working Austin/Lakeway/Dripping Springs with condo and single-family transactions. Occupation and employer strings in the filing match exactly. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration and no federal PAC record surfaced under this name; a separate 'Jonathan Friedman' on the Northwest Hills (NWACA) board is a different person and was excluded.

organizations.role: Member, Real Estate - Commercial directory listing
organizations.notes: Perry Lorenz is a 50-year Austin resident who has worked primarily as a principal in the purchase, sale, development and management of downtown Austin real estate since 1977, forming and serving as managing general partner of numerous downtown development partnerships. With partners he has developed 1,000+ condominium units in mixed-use projects including Spring, BartonPlace, Este, The Pedernales, Saltillo Lofts and The Independent, and controlled full city blocks where several of downtown Austin's tallest towers now stand (including the site of the 1,022-ft Waterline). His Austin Chamber listing (Real Estate - Commercial, 1311-A E. Sixth St.) matches the East Austin office described in coverage of the Mitchell family. Zip 78703 (Old West Austin/Clarksville) is consistent. The filing's employer 'Mitchell Family Corp' is corroborated: Lorenz is the husband of Sheridan Mitchell Lorenz, daughter of the late Texas oil and gas billionaire George P. Mitchell, and father of Katherine Lorenz, president of the Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation; Mitchell Family Corp is the family's Austin/The Woodlands family office. Note: Mitchell Family Corp is a family office and philanthropic-support entity, not an operating oil & gas company (Mitchell Energy & Development was sold to Devon Energy in 2002), so no oil_gas flag is asserted for Lorenz himself.

organizations.org: Mitchell Family Corporation / Mitchell Historic Properties
organizations.notes: Sheridan Mitchell Lorenz, Austin TX 78703 — corroborated by FEC individual-contribution records listing 'Sheridan Lorenz, Austin TX 78703, employer Mitchell Family Corp.' She is a daughter of Cynthia and George P. Mitchell (Mitchell Energy & Development; credited with pioneering commercial shale-gas extraction), past vice president of the Mitchell Family Corporation and of Mitchell Historic Properties (Galveston real estate), president/director of G-1 Corporation, VP and managing member of Circle Ten Inc., and a director of Mitchell Island Investments. She is a board member of the Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation, which funds climate-change, renewable-energy, aquifer-protection and land-conservation work. Her personal wealth derives from an oil-and-gas fortune, but no public record shows her holding an executive, board, or ownership role in an operating oil & gas company, so no oil_gas flag is recorded.

Employer 'CCRG' resolves to Corpus Christi Realty Group, where Victoria (Munt) Rogers is a listed agent; her Padre Island Business Association directory entry gives a Corpus Christi 78418 address (Grenadine Dr), matching the donor zip exactly, and her personal site is padreisland78418.com.

Gregory Keith Wooldridge, 65 (b. 1961), West Lake Hills TX, co-owns 501 Rocky River Road, West Lake Hills 78746 with his wife Lynne Dobson — matching the filing's 78746 / West Lake Hills / 'Retired, Self'. He is a retired newspaperman (Austin American-Statesman sports and features editor) who with Dobson founded the Tejemos Foundation, a major Austin philanthropic funder. NOT the Blue Angels commander: that Greg 'Boss' Wooldridge is a retired Navy captain, roughly 78, based in Oregon, where he ran for governor in 2018 — different age, state, and career.

Conor Kenny is a principal at Capital A Housing, an Austin affordable-housing development consultancy; the self-reported occupation 'affordable housing builder' and Austin 78723 (East Austin) location match. He chaired the City of Austin Planning Commission (served 2017-2021, stepping down as chair in Aug. 2020 to work with Fayez Kazi on affordable housing) and served on the City of Austin Design Commission 2016-2021. Earlier career: investigative researcher on money in politics for Public Citizen in Washington, DC, and editor of Congresspedia.org. No TEC lobbyist registration surfaced; no PAC or advocacy tie on the Israel/Palestine, oil & gas, gun, or defense spectrums.

organizations.org: Carmen Llanes Pulido for Mayor of Austin (2024)
organizations.notes: Carmen Llanes Pulido, native East Austinite (donor zip 78722 is East Austin), has been Executive Director of Go Austin/Vamos Austin (GAVA) since March 2017 and ran for Mayor of Austin in 2024, finishing second with about 20% of the vote. Filing employer/occupation ('Go Austin Vamos Austin (GAVA)', 'Executive Director / Nonprofit Executive Director') matches exactly. She previously organized with PODER (People Organized in Defense of Earth and her Resources) in East Austin and ran a fitness/nutrition program at Marathon Kids. FEC search returned no federal PAC contributions under her name; her documented political finance activity is her local Austin mayoral committee (~$71,000 raised through July 2024), which is city-level, not federal. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration under 'Llanes' was found. Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile, InfluenceWatch and UT School of Architecture event bio found extensive local civic and land-use advocacy but no Israel/Palestine, gun, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations on either side of those spectrums.

She was appointed to the South-Western City Schools Board of Education in 2024.

Her documented advocacy is environmental justice, anti-gentrification and land use in East Austin; no federal PAC, lobbyist-registry, or Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas, or defense-industry affiliations were found on either side of those spectrums.

She is a board member of the Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation, which funds climate-change, renewable-energy, aquifer-protection and land-conservation work. Her personal wealth derives from an oil-and-gas fortune, but no public record shows her holding an executive, board, or ownership role in an operating oil & gas company, so no oil_gas flag is recorded.

Sue Marriott, LCSW, CGP, is a self-employed Austin psychotherapist in private practice and co-founder of Therapist Uncensored, matching the filing's 'Therapist'/'Self' occupation and Austin location. Directory listings show a 78703 practice address rather than the filing's 78751 residential zip, so the zip is not directly corroborated.

Gregory Keith Wooldridge, 65 (b. 1961), West Lake Hills TX, co-owns 501 Rocky River Road, West Lake Hills 78746 with his wife Lynne Dobson — matching the filing's 78746 / West Lake Hills / 'Retired, Self'. He is a retired newspaperman (Austin American-Statesman sports and features editor) who with Dobson founded the Tejemos Foundation, a major Austin philanthropic funder. NOT the Blue Angels commander: that Greg 'Boss' Wooldridge is a retired Navy captain, roughly 78, based in Oregon, where he ran for governor in 2018 — different age, state, and career.

Virginia Marie Raymond (JD, PhD; Texas bar since 1985) is a solo immigration and civil-rights practitioner in Austin 78722 — an exact zip match, consistent with occupation 'lawyer' / employer 'self-employed'. Her office address is shared with Thomas L. Kolker, who made an identical $400 gift to Anderson on the same date, corroborating a shared household. FEC PAC search surfaced no ideological-PAC contributions attributable to her (fec_donation_count 88 could not be resolved to specific committees via public search); no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found.

Nadia Barbot is an Austin technology product manager (Group Product Manager at Sysco LABS; formerly Bazaarvoice) who serves as Secretary of the Pecan Springs-Springdale Hills Neighborhood Association — the association covering the 78723 Mueller-area zip on the filing, which corroborates the identity directly. Her heavy federal donation history (77 contributions, lean 1.0) is consistent with her documented gun-safety advocacy; no individual PAC-level records were retrievable through public search tools, and no TEC lobbyist registration was found.

Filing lists occupation 'Unemployed' / employer 'None' in 78705 (West Campus/Hyde Park). A Rachel Lomas of Austin is publicly listed as a director of Midterms Matter, an Austin group organizing young-voter turnout, and appears with Andy Bowman as host of a fundraiser for U.S. Rep. Lloyd Doggett — consistent with the 193 federal contributions and uniformly Democratic partisan lean in the donation record. Identity is corroborated by Austin location and the heavy small-dollar Democratic giving pattern, but not by an employer, so confidence is medium rather than high.

Jennifer Vickers is the president and co-founder of Community Investment Corporation, an Austin nonprofit she started with her husband Lee Walker, working on environmental initiatives, microcredit and entrepreneurial support, and public-space/urban design; she holds a BA from Brown and an MBA from UT Austin. Her unpaid civic/board work is consistent with the 'Volunteer / Self' occupation on the contribution, and her long-running Austin civic footprint plus the heavily Democratic federal giving pattern corroborate the match, though I could not independently confirm the 78751 address from a primary source.

Longtime Austin civic figure and philanthropist who runs MDW Interests, a private oil, gas and real estate investment firm registered in Austin. Co-founder and president of the Waller Creek Conservancy (now Waterloo Greenway), former facilities chair at St. Edward's University, and instrumental in the merger creating The Contemporary Austin. Despite the 'Unemployed / None' occupation on the filing, her business identity is documented in public press releases. Her 213 federal contributions with a near-uniform Democratic lean are consistent with the civic-donor profile; no ideological PAC tie (pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, gun, or defense) was found in public records.

Ted Whatley of Austin is described in a Texas Monthly feature as a history teacher; he and his wife Melba Whatley built a 2,400-sq-ft private library attached to their Tarrytown home, and 78703 is the Tarrytown ZIP, matching the filing's zip, 'teacher' occupation and 'Retired' employer. He is also listed as a past board trustee of the Austin education nonprofit Breakthrough Central Texas. FEC PAC search surfaced no federal PAC contributions under Ted/Theodore Whatley in Austin, and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration was found.

Best match is George Cofer, sixth-generation Austinite and founding CEO of the Hill Country Conservancy (1999-2021), who retired in 2021 — consistent with the filing's self-employed 'consultant / project manager' occupation. Corroborating: he is a longtime Save Barton Creek Association activist, and the 78746 zip (West Lake Hills / Rollingwood / Barton Creek) is that organization's core area; the donor's heavy Democratic federal giving pattern (144 contributions, 0.964 partisan lean) fits an Austin environmental Democrat. Not confirmed against a residential address, so medium rather than high. No TEC lobbyist registration found; Hill Country Conservancy appears in the City of Austin lobbyist database as a client entry, not as a state lobbyist registration for Cofer personally. No ideological-PAC giving surfaced on either side of any tracked spectrum.

Conor Kenny is a principal at Capital A Housing, an Austin affordable-housing development consultancy; the self-reported occupation 'affordable housing builder' and Austin 78723 (East Austin) location match. He chaired the City of Austin Planning Commission (served 2017-2021, stepping down as chair in Aug. 2020 to work with Fayez Kazi on affordable housing) and served on the City of Austin Design Commission 2016-2021. Earlier career: investigative researcher on money in politics for Public Citizen in Washington, DC, and editor of Congresspedia.org. No TEC lobbyist registration surfaced; no PAC or advocacy tie on the Israel/Palestine, oil & gas, gun, or defense spectrums.

Lucia Athens served 12 years as the City of Austin's first Chief Sustainability Officer and retired from City service in January 2023; she now works as an author and speaker (book 'The Sustainability Revolutionists'). Her self-reported occupation of 'Unemployed'/employer 'None' on the April 2026 contribution is consistent with that retirement, and the distinctive name plus Austin residence corroborate identity. Earlier career: led Seattle's green building program for 10 years; MS in Architectural Studies (sustainable design), UT Austin.

organizations.role: President and CEO (former; first female president)
organizations.notes: Confirmed as Dr. Colette Pierce Burnette. The employer string 'WGC' resolves to Waterloo Greenway Conservancy, which named her CEO effective April 1, 2025; the CEO title in the filing matches exactly. She is the former (and first female) president of Huston-Tillotson University in Austin and was previously a Waterloo Greenway board member. Austin residency and the CEO title corroborate the 78703 filing.

Prominent Austin photographer, author and transit advocate; UT Law grad and former corporate attorney who served on the Capital Metro board and other city/county boards. Fits the donation pattern exactly (gave to county Democrats Travillion & Ann Howard; 0.91 Dem FEC lean, 32 FEC gifts). No zip on file to fully confirm, but identity/donor profile is a strong match.

Carmen Llanes Pulido, native East Austinite (donor zip 78722 is East Austin), has been Executive Director of Go Austin/Vamos Austin (GAVA) since March 2017 and ran for Mayor of Austin in 2024, finishing second with about 20% of the vote. Filing employer/occupation ('Go Austin Vamos Austin (GAVA)', 'Executive Director / Nonprofit Executive Director') matches exactly. She previously organized with PODER (People Organized in Defense of Earth and her Resources) in East Austin and ran a fitness/nutrition program at Marathon Kids. FEC search returned no federal PAC contributions under her name; her documented political finance activity is her local Austin mayoral committee (~$71,000 raised through July 2024), which is city-level, not federal. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration under 'Llanes' was found. Full bio read of the Austin Monitor profile, InfluenceWatch and UT School of Architecture event bio found extensive local civic and land-use advocacy but no Israel/Palestine, gun, oil-and-gas or defense affiliations on either side of those spectrums.

organizations.org: Central Ohio Area Agency on Aging (COAAA) Regional Council of Governments
organizations.notes: Camille Peterson is a licensed social worker (LSW since 2002, BSSW/MSW from Ohio State) employed by COAAA, the Central Ohio Area Agency on Aging, and has lived in Galloway, Ohio for over 20 years — matching zip 43119, the 'Social Worker' occupation, and the 'Council of Government' employer, since COAAA converted to a Regional Council of Governments effective January 1, 2026, months before the June 2026 gift. She was appointed to the South-Western City Schools Board of Education in 2024.

organizations.org: Southwest Network for Environmental and Economic Justice
organizations.role: Helped found; co-chair of the Coordinating Council
organizations.notes: Reported occupation 'Director' and employer 'PODER' match exactly a well-documented East Austin public-record figure: Susana Almanza co-founded PODER in 1991 and serves as its director. She is a longtime environmental-justice organizer, a two-time Austin City Council District 3 candidate (2014, 2018), and a 2021 appointee to the National Environmental Justice Advisory Council. Her documented advocacy is environmental justice, anti-gentrification and land use in East Austin; no federal PAC, lobbyist-registry, or Israel/Palestine, firearms, oil-and-gas, or defense-industry affiliations were found on either side of those spectrums.
